14: // configuration Contains information from the labels that are globals (not related to the dynamic configuration)
15: // or specific to the provider.
16: type configuration struct {
17: 	Enable bool
18: 	Docker specificConfiguration
19: }

26: func (p *Shared) extractLabels(container dockerData) (configuration, error) {
27: 	conf := configuration{
28: 		Enable: p.ExposedByDefault,
29: 		Docker: specificConfiguration{
30: 			Network: p.Network,
31: 		},
32: 	}
33: 
34: 	err := label.Decode(container.Labels, &conf, "traefik.docker.", "traefik.enable")
35: 	if err != nil {
36: 		return configuration{}, err
37: 	}
38: 
39: 	return conf, nil
40: }

42: // getStringMultipleStrict get multiple string values associated to several labels.
43: // Fail if one label is missing.
44: func getStringMultipleStrict(labels map[string]string, labelNames ...string) (map[string]string, error) {
45: 	foundLabels := map[string]string{}
46: 	for _, name := range labelNames {
47: 		value := getStringValue(labels, name, "")
48: 		// Error out only if one of them is not defined.
49: 		if len(value) == 0 {
50: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("label not found: %s", name)
51: 		}
52: 		foundLabels[name] = value
53: 	}
54: 	return foundLabels, nil
55: }
56: 
57: // getStringValue get string value associated to a label.
58: func getStringValue(labels map[string]string, labelName, defaultValue string) string {
59: 	if value, ok := labels[labelName]; ok && len(value) > 0 {
60: 		return value
61: 	}
62: 	return defaultValue

25: 			tasks: []swarm.Task{

29: 					taskStatus(taskState(swarm.TaskStateRunning)),
30: 				),
31: 				swarmTask("id2",
32: 					taskSlot(2),
33: 					taskNetworkAttachment("1", "network1", "overlay", []string{"127.0.0.2"}),
34: 					taskStatus(taskState(swarm.TaskStatePending)),
35: 				),
36: 				swarmTask("id3",
37: 					taskSlot(3),
38: 					taskNetworkAttachment("1", "network1", "overlay", []string{"127.0.0.3"}),
39: 				),
40: 				swarmTask("id4",
41: 					taskSlot(4),
42: 					taskNetworkAttachment("1", "network1", "overlay", []string{"127.0.0.4"}),
43: 					taskStatus(taskState(swarm.TaskStateRunning)),
44: 				),
45: 				swarmTask("id5",
46: 					taskSlot(5),
47: 					taskNetworkAttachment("1", "network1", "overlay", []string{"127.0.0.5"}),
48: 					taskStatus(taskState(swarm.TaskStateFailed)),
49: 				),

51: 			isGlobalSVC: false,
52: 			expectedTasks: []string{
53: 				"container.1",
54: 				"container.4",

58: 					Name: "foo",

60: 			},
61: 		},
62: 	}
63: 
64: 	for caseID, test := range testCases {
65: 		t.Run(strconv.Itoa(caseID), func(t *testing.T) {

73: 			taskDockerData, _ := listTasks(context.Background(), dockerClient, test.service.ID, dockerData, test.networks, test.isGlobalSVC)

75: 			if len(test.expectedTasks) != len(taskDockerData) {

79: 			for i, taskID := range test.expectedTasks {

81: 					t.Errorf("expect task id %v, got %v", taskID, taskDockerData[i].Name)
82: 				}
83: 			}
84: 		})
85: 	}
86: }

1: // Package label implements the decoding and encoding between flat labels and a typed Configuration.
2: package label
3: 
4: import (
5: 	"github.com/traefik/paerser/parser"
6: 	"github.com/traefik/traefik/v3/pkg/config/dynamic"
7: )
8: 
9: // DecodeConfiguration converts the labels to a configuration.
10: func DecodeConfiguration(labels map[string]string) (*dynamic.Configuration, error) {
11: 	conf := &dynamic.Configuration{
12: 		HTTP: &dynamic.HTTPConfiguration{},
13: 		TCP:  &dynamic.TCPConfiguration{},
14: 		UDP:  &dynamic.UDPConfiguration{},
15: 		TLS:  &dynamic.TLSConfiguration{},
16: 	}
17: 
18: 	// When decoding the TLS configuration we are making sure that only the default TLS store can be configured.
19: 	err := parser.Decode(labels, conf, parser.DefaultRootName, "traefik.http", "traefik.tcp", "traefik.udp", "traefik.tls.stores.default")
20: 	if err != nil {
21: 		return nil, err
22: 	}
23: 
24: 	return conf, nil
25: }
26: 
27: // EncodeConfiguration converts a configuration to labels.
28: func EncodeConfiguration(conf *dynamic.Configuration) (map[string]string, error) {
29: 	return parser.Encode(conf, parser.DefaultRootName)
30: }
31: 
32: // Decode converts the labels to an element.
33: // labels -> [ node -> node + metadata (type) ] -> element (node).
34: func Decode(labels map[string]string, element interface{}, filters ...string) error {
35: 	return parser.Decode(labels, element, parser.DefaultRootName, filters...)
36: }

(End of file - total 36 lines)
